findings.evidence: `rg` shows `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/LagrangianGraph.lean:138: lemma isIsotropic_graph_iff {e : V ≃ₗ[ℝ] W}` and Mathlib already defines `LinearMap.graph` generally at `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Prod.lean:873`; the proof lines 143-150 only use graph membership and evaluation of `e`, not `e.symm` or bijectivity.
findings.fix: State and prove the general lemma for `{f : V →ₗ[ℝ] W}`: `(twistedProd ω₁ ω₂).IsIsotropic f.graph ↔ ∀ v w, ω₂ (f v) (f w) = ω₁ v w`, then derive the current symplectomorphism version for `e.toLinearMap`.
findings.issue: `isIsotropic_graph_iff` assumes `{e : V ≃ₗ[ℝ] W}` even though graph isotropy only uses the linear map and the pointwise identity `ω₂ (f v) (f w) = ω₁ v w`.

summary: The isotropic graph API is stated at the wrong level: isotropy of a graph only needs a linear map preserving the forms, not a linear equivalence. The Lagrangian equivalence can remain as the symplectomorphism corollary.
